edible_parts: Fruit, flower buds, and young pads (all cooked/eaten).
other_uses: Spineless-looking blue-gray prickly pear (armed with tiny glochids) with brilliant magenta flowers.
prep: Roast the flower buds, eat the fruit, and cook the young pads (nopales); a desert food — but beware the fine barbed glochids, which must be burned or scraped off first.
soil: Dry, rocky, sharply drained desert soils.
risks: Tiny barbed glochids — remove carefully before eating.
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
